title: Remotely from Georgia Program
summary: Georgia offers a unique program allowing digital nomads and remote workers to live in Georgia for up to 1 year (or extend). US citizens can stay 365 days visa-free, and there's no income tax on foreign-sourced income for participants in the Remotely from Georgia program.
steps: Enter Georgia with US passport (365-day visa-free stay for US citizens)
Register your stay online with the Public Service Development Agency within 15 days
Open a Georgian bank account (Bank of Georgia, TBC Bank) for daily transactions
Apply for 'Remotely from Georgia' status at service areas (service.gov.ge)
Provide proof of remote employment or self-employment
Obtain Georgian ID card (optional but helpful for longer activities)
Extend stay if needed before 365 days
documents: Proof of remote employment (employment contract, letter from employer)
Proof of self-employment (business registration, client contracts, invoices)
Proof of accommodation (hotel booking, rental contract, Airbnb receipts)
fee: Free visa-free entry; Remotely from Georgia registration fee approximately 50 GEL ($20 USD)
notes: The Remotely from Georgia program offers: no income tax on foreign-sourced income, 1-year visa-free stay, ability to open bank accounts, and access to Georgian healthcare system. The 15-day registration is important to avoid fines.
